title: Auto-FDE skill catalog manifest
description: Single source of truth for a team-plugin project. Written by /fde-plan, statused by every later phase.

team.description: Team name as they say it, e.g. 'Acme Launch Team'

phases.description: The team's own workflow phases, used to group skills (e.g. Anytime, Intake, Production, Wrap).

lanes.description: Optional role lanes (e.g. PM, Creative). Omit if the team has one audience.

type.description: document = produces a team artifact from a template; workflow = guides a multi-step process; integration = wraps an external tool/MCP.

summary.description: One-line planning summary. NOT the final SKILL.md description — that is authored at build time against the doctrine.

digests.description: Digest files (relative to project root) the build agent must read for this skill.

sources.description: Raw source files backing this skill, for fidelity checks. Never bundled.

templates.description: Blank template files to bundle into <plugin>/templates/ and wire into the skill.

success.description: Must-pass criteria written at plan time (outcome/process/style/efficiency). Build compiles these into the skill's deterministic evals/checks.json.

composes.description: Slugs of other catalog skills this one invokes.

vendor.description: True if provided by a third-party plugin (listed for planning, not built).

integrations.description: External tools/MCP servers the plugin depends on, with auth notes.

mcp.description: When present, build bundles this server into the plugin's .mcp.json instead of leaving it as manual setup. Secrets stay ${ENV_VAR} references, documented in the plugin README.

command.description: stdio: the launch command; use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for bundled paths.

env.description: Env vars the server needs, values as ${ENV_VAR} placeholders — never literal secrets.

hooks.description: Deterministic gates the team's workflow needs (the 'determinism belongs in code' cases). Build scaffolds these into hooks/hooks.json.

event.description: Hook event, e.g. PostToolUse, PreToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop.

matcher.description: Tool/source matcher where the event supports one.

type.description: command = a script decides; prompt = the model is instructed.

purpose.description: What the gate enforces, in the team's words — e.g. 'lint every edited brief against the canonical headings'.
